report_name: Black Shrantac Ransomware Targets Industrial Sector with Double Extortion and Living-off-the-Land Tactics

threat_actors.name: Black Shrantac
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group active since September 2025 that employs a double extortion strategy against various industries, including manufacturing and the public sector. The group is known for exploiting public-facing vulnerabilities and using living-off-the-land (LOTL) tactics.
threat_actors.goals: Financial gain through double extortion, involving data encryption and the threat of leaking exfiltrated data.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The Black Shrantac group gained initial access by exploiting CVE-2024-3400, a command injection vulnerability in PAN-OS GlobalProtect gateways.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor abused PowerShell as part of its living-off-the-land (LOTL) tactics to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Service Execution
attack_patterns.description: The group used legitimate tools like PsExec to execute processes on remote systems for lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The group utilized RDP to move laterally within victim networks after gaining initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Black Shrantac transferred legitimate administrative tools like PsExec between systems to facilitate lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: As part of their living-off-the-land tactics, the group likely performed OS credential dumping to escalate privileges and move laterally, although the specific method was not detailed.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Black Shrantac ransomware payload was executed to encrypt critical files and systems, causing operational disruption as the final stage of the attack.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Before deploying ransomware, the group exfiltrated large volumes of high-value data to be used as leverage in their double extortion scheme.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
attack_patterns.description: The group likely leveraged WMI as part of their living-off-the-land techniques to execute commands and move through the victim environment.

malware.name: Black Shrantac Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the Black Shrantac group to encrypt victim files and systems as part of a double extortion scheme.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Abused by the Black Shrantac group as a living-off-the-land tool to move between workstations and execute commands.

tools.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
tools.description: Used by the attackers for lateral movement within compromised networks.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2024-3400
vulnerabilities.description: A critical command injection vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect gateways, exploited by Black Shrantac for initial access.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions as they become available. Otherwise, users with vulnerable versions of affected devices should enable Threat Prevention IDs available from the vendor. See the vendor bulletin for more details and a patch release schedule.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS Command Injection Vulnerability

identities.name: Manufacturing Sector
identities.description: The manufacturing industry was identified as a target of the Black Shrantac ransomware group.

identities.name: Public Sector
identities.description: The public sector was identified as a target of the Black Shrantac ransomware group.

infrastructure.name: Black Shrantac Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A Tor-based leak site used by the Black Shrantac group to publish stolen data from victims who do not pay the ransom.

indicators.name: PsExec Execution for Lateral Movement
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PsExec Execution
id: 2b9a7f3c-1d9e-4a8b-9c6d-0f1e2d3c4b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of PsExec to execute commands on a remote host, which can be indicative of lateral movement by threat actors like Black Shrantac.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/attacks-and-vulnerabilities/black-shrantac-exposes-industrial-environments-to-stealth-ransomware-risk-through-lotl-double-extortion-tactics/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1569.002
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\PSEXESVC.exe'
    ParentImage|endswith: '\services.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the PsExec service executable, which is created on a remote host during PsExec execution. This was a technique used by Black Shrantac.

indicators.name: CVE-2024-3400 Exploitation Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Palo Alto PAN-OS CVE-2024-3400 Exploitation Attempt
id: 8c4d5e6f-7b8a-4c9d-8e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
status: experimental
description: Detects web requests to '/ssl-vpn/hip-report.esp', a URI pattern associated with the exploitation of CVE-2024-3400 in PAN-OS.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/attacks-and-vulnerabilities/black-shrantac-exposes-industrial-environments-to-stealth-ransomware-risk-through-lotl-double-extortion-tactics/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
level: critical
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/ssl-vpn/hip-report.esp'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests matching the pattern for CVE-2024-3400 exploitation, which Black Shrantac used for initial access.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Outbound Data Transfer
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Outbound Data Transfer
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects large volumes of outbound data over non-standard ports, which could indicate data exfiltration prior to a ransomware attack, as seen with Black Shrantac.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/attacks-and-vulnerabilities/black-shrantac-exposes-industrial-environments-to-stealth-ransomware-risk-through-lotl-double-extortion-tactics/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1041
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    action: 'allow'
    bytes_out|gt: 100000000 # 100MB
  filter_known_ports:
    dst_port:
      - 80
      - 443
      - 22
      - 21
  condition: selection and not filter_known_ports
indicators.description: Detects large outbound data transfers, a key TTP for the Black Shrantac group's double extortion model.
